theorem_statement:
Finite-time blow-up from a virial identity for focusing mass-critical NLS.

Let \(u(t,x)\) be a sufficiently regular solution on \(\mathbb{R}^d\) with finite variance. Assume:

1. mass is conserved:

\[
\|u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}=\|u_0\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}=:M>0,
\]

2. energy is conserved and negative:

\[
E(u(t))=E(u_0)<0,
\]

3. the variance

\[
V(t)=\int_{\mathbb{R}^d}|x|^2|u(t,x)|^2\,dx
\]

satisfies

\[
V''(t)=16E(u_0)<0,
\]

4. for every sufficiently regular \(f\),

\[
\|f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}^2
\le
\frac{2}{d}\,\|x f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\,\|\nabla f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}.
\]

Then there exists \(T<\infty\) such that

\[
\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\to\infty
\quad\text{as } t\uparrow T.
\]

nodes.title: Finite-time blow-up from negative virial curvature
nodes.informal_statement: There exists \(T<\infty\) such that \(\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\to\infty\) as \(t\uparrow T\).
nodes.informal_proof_text:
Use the finite-time vanishing of the variance from \(n1\), together with the lower bound on \(\|
abla u(t)\|_{L^2}\) in terms of \(V(t)\) from \(n2\). Since \(M>0\) is fixed and \(\sqrt{V(t)}	o 0\) as \(t\uparrow T\), the inequality \(M^2\le  rac{2}{d}\sqrt{V(t)}\,\|
abla u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\) can remain valid only if \(\|
abla u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}	o\infty\).

nodes.title: The variance reaches zero in finite positive time
nodes.informal_statement: There exists a finite \(T>0\) such that \(V(T)=0\), hence \(V(t)\to 0\) as \(t\uparrow T\).
nodes.informal_proof_text: Because \(E(u_0)<0\), the virial identity gives \(V''(t)=16E(u_0)<0\) for all times of existence. Integrating twice, \(V\) is a quadratic polynomial \(V(t)=8E(u_0)t^2+at+b\) with negative leading coefficient, so \(V(t)\to -\infty\) as \(t\to +\infty\). On the other hand, by definition \(V(t)=\int_{\mathbb{R}^d}|x|^2|u(t,x)|^2\,dx\ge 0\). Therefore \(V\) cannot stay positive for all \(t\ge 0\). By continuity, it must hit zero at some finite time \(T>0\), and thus \(V(t)\to 0\) as \(t\uparrow T\).

nodes.title: Mass and the weighted inequality force a gradient lower bound
nodes.informal_statement: For every time in the interval of existence, \[M^2=\|u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}^2\le \frac{2}{d}\sqrt{V(t)}\,\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}.\] Equivalently, \[\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\ge \frac{d}{2}\,\frac{M^2}{\sqrt{V(t)}}.\]
nodes.informal_proof_text: Apply the assumed inequality \(\|f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}^2\le \frac{2}{d}\,\|x f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\,\|\nabla f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\) with \(f=u(t)\). Mass conservation gives \(\|u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}=M\), so \(M^2\le \frac{2}{d}\,\|x u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\,\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\). Using \(\|x u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}^2=V(t)\), we obtain \(\|x u(t)\|_{L^2}=\sqrt{V(t)}\), hence the stated inequality.

edges.label: depends_on
edges.explanation: The blow-up time is obtained from the finite positive time at which the variance collapses to zero.

edges.label: depends_on
edges.explanation: The divergence of \(\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2}\) follows by combining the gradient lower bound with \(V(t)\to 0\).
